lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਲਸਕਰ ਲਖ ਵਾਜੇ ਨੇਜੇ ਲਖ ਉਠਿ ਕਰਹਿ ਸਲਾਮੁ ॥
lines.translit: lakh lasakar lakh vaaje neje lakh utth kareh salaam |
lines.english: You may have thousands of armies, thousands of marching bands and lances, and thousands of men to rise and salute you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਘਰੁ ੩ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa ghar 3 mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, Third House, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖਾ ਉਪਰਿ ਫੁਰਮਾਇਸਿ ਤੇਰੀ ਲਖ ਉਠਿ ਰਾਖਹਿ ਮਾਨੁ ॥
lines.translit: lakhaa upar furamaaeis teree lakh utth raakheh maan |
lines.english: Your rule may extend over thousands of miles, and thousands of men may rise to honor you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਪਤਿ ਲੇਖੈ ਨਾ ਪਵੈ ਤਾਂ ਸਭਿ ਨਿਰਾਫਲ ਕਾਮ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jaan pat lekhai naa pavai taan sabh niraafal kaam |1|
lines.english: But, if your honor is of no account to the Lord, then all of your ostentatious show is useless. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਬਹੁਤਾ ਸਮਝਾਈਐ ਭੋਲਾ ਭੀ ਸੋ ਅੰਧੋ ਅੰਧਾ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: je bahutaa samajhaaeeai bholaa bhee so andho andhaa |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Even though the fool may be taught again and again, he remains the blindest of the blind. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਖਟੀਅਹਿ ਲਖ ਸੰਜੀਅਹਿ ਖਾਜਹਿ ਲਖ ਆਵਹਿ ਲਖ ਜਾਹਿ ॥
lines.translit: lakh khatteeeh lakh sanjeeeh khaajeh lakh aaveh lakh jaeh |
lines.english: You may earn thousands, collect thousands, and spend thousands of dollars; thousands may come, and thousands may go.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਰਿ ਕੇ ਨਾਮ ਬਿਨਾ ਜਗੁ ਧੰਧਾ ॥
lines.translit: har ke naam binaa jag dhandhaa |
lines.english: Without the Name of the Lord, the world is in turmoil.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਪਤਿ ਲੇਖੈ ਨਾ ਪਵੈ ਤਾਂ ਜੀਅ ਕਿਥੈ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਾਹਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jaan pat lekhai naa pavai taan jeea kithai fir paeh |2|
lines.english: But, if your honor is of no account to the Lord, then where will you go to find a safe haven? ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਸਾਸਤ ਸਮਝਾਵਣੀ ਲਖ ਪੰਡਿਤ ਪੜਹਿ ਪੁਰਾਣ ॥
lines.translit: lakh saasat samajhaavanee lakh panddit parreh puraan |
lines.english: Thousands of Shaastras may be explained to the mortal, and thousands of Pandits may read the Puraanas to him;

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਾਂ ਪਤਿ ਲੇਖੈ ਨਾ ਪਵੈ ਤਾਂ ਸਭੇ ਕੁਪਰਵਾਣ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: jaan pat lekhai naa pavai taan sabhe kuparavaan |3|
lines.english: but, if his honor is of no account to the Lord, then all of this is unacceptable. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਜੇ ਵਸੈ ਨਾਨਕ ਨਦਰੀ ਪਾਰੁ ॥੪॥੧॥੩੧॥
lines.translit: ahinis hiradai je vasai naanak nadaree paar |4|1|31|
lines.english: If it abides in the heart, day and night, O Nanak, then the mortal shall swim across, by His Grace. ||4||1||31||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚ ਨਾਮਿ ਪਤਿ ਊਪਜੈ ਕਰਮਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: sach naam pat aoopajai karam naam karataar |
lines.english: Honor comes from the True Name, the Name of the Merciful Creator.

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਨਿ ਚਾਨਣਿ ਓਹੁ ਸੋਖਿਆ ਚੂਕਾ ਜਮ ਸਿਉ ਮੇਲੁ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: aun chaanan ohu sokhiaa chookaa jam siau mel |1|
lines.english: Its flame has dried up this oil, and I have escaped my meeting with the Messenger of Death. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੀਵਾ ਮੇਰਾ ਏਕੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਦੁਖੁ ਵਿਚਿ ਪਾਇਆ ਤੇਲੁ ॥
lines.translit: deevaa meraa ek naam dukh vich paaeaa tel |
lines.english: The One Name is my lamp; I have put the oil of suffering into it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲੋਕਾ ਮਤ ਕੋ ਫਕੜਿ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: lokaa mat ko fakarr paae |
lines.english: O people, do not make fun of me.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਮੜਿਆ ਕਰਿ ਏਕਠੇ ਏਕ ਰਤੀ ਲੇ ਭਾਹਿ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: lakh marriaa kar ekatthe ek ratee le bhaeh |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Thousands of wooden logs, piled up together, need only a tiny flame to burn. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੰਗ ਬਨਾਰਸਿ ਸਿਫਤਿ ਤੁਮਾਰੀ ਨਾਵੈ ਆਤਮ ਰਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: gang banaaras sifat tumaaree naavai aatam raau |
lines.english: The Lord's Praise is my River Ganges and my city of Benares; my soul takes its sacred cleansing bath there.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਿੰਡੁ ਪਤਲਿ ਮੇਰੀ ਕੇਸਉ ਕਿਰਿਆ ਸਚੁ ਨਾਮੁ ਕਰਤਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: pindd patal meree kesau kiriaa sach naam karataar |
lines.english: The Lord is my festive dish, of rice balls on leafy plates; the True Name of the Creator Lord is my funeral ceremony.

lines.gurmukhi: ਐਥੈ ਓਥੈ ਆਗੈ ਪਾਛੈ ਏਹੁ ਮੇਰਾ ਆਧਾਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: aithai othai aagai paachhai ehu meraa aadhaar |2|
lines.english: Here and hereafter, in the past and in the future, this is my support. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਇਕ ਲੋਕੀ ਹੋਰੁ ਛਮਿਛਰੀ ਬ੍ਰਾਹਮਣੁ ਵਟਿ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: eik lokee hor chhamichharee braahaman vatt pindd khaae |
lines.english: The rice balls are offered to the gods and the dead ancestors, but it is the Brahmins who eat them!

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚਾ ਨਾਵਣੁ ਤਾਂ ਥੀਐ ਜਾਂ ਅਹਿਨਿਸਿ ਲਾਗੈ ਭਾਉ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: sachaa naavan taan theeai jaan ahinis laagai bhaau |3|
lines.english: That becomes my true cleansing bath, if night and day, I enshrine love for You. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਬਖਸੀਸ ਕਾ ਕਬਹੂੰ ਨਿਖੂਟਸਿ ਨਾਹਿ ॥੪॥੨॥੩੨॥
lines.translit: naanak pindd bakhasees kaa kabahoon nikhoottas naeh |4|2|32|
lines.english: O Nanak, the rice balls of the Lord are a gift which is never exhausted. ||4||2||32||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਸਾ ਘਰੁ ੪ ਮਹਲਾ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: aasaa ghar 4 mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Aasaa, Fourth House, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ੴ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਪ੍ਰਸਾਦਿ ॥
lines.translit: ik oankaar satigur prasaad |
lines.english: One Universal Creator God. By The Grace Of The True Guru:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇਵਤਿਆ ਦਰਸਨ ਕੈ ਤਾਈ ਦੂਖ ਭੂਖ ਤੀਰਥ ਕੀਏ ॥
lines.translit: devatiaa darasan kai taaee dookh bhookh teerath kee |
lines.english: The Gods, yearning for the Blessed Vision of the Lord's Darshan, suffered through pain and hunger at the sacred shrines.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋਗੀ ਜਤੀ ਜੁਗਤਿ ਮਹਿ ਰਹਤੇ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਭਗਵੇ ਭੇਖ ਭਏ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jogee jatee jugat meh rahate kar kar bhagave bhekh bhe |1|
lines.english: The yogis and the celibates live their disciplined lifestyle, while others wear saffron robes and become hermits. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਉ ਕਾਰਣਿ ਸਾਹਿਬਾ ਰੰਗਿ ਰਤੇ ॥
lines.translit: tau kaaran saahibaa rang rate |
lines.english: For Your sake, O Lord Master, they are imbued with love.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੇ ਨਾਮ ਅਨੇਕਾ ਰੂਪ ਅਨੰਤਾ ਕਹਣੁ ਨ ਜਾਹੀ ਤੇਰੇ ਗੁਣ ਕੇਤੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: tere naam anekaa roop anantaa kehan na jaahee tere gun kete |1| rahaau |
lines.english: Your Names are so many, and Your Forms are endless. No one can tell how may Glorious Virtues You have. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪੀਰ ਪੇਕਾਂਬਰ ਸਾਲਿਕ ਸਾਦਿਕ ਛੋਡੀ ਦੁਨੀਆ ਥਾਇ ਪਏ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: peer pekaanbar saalik saadik chhoddee duneea thaae pe |2|
lines.english: The spiritual leaders, prophets, seers and men of faith renounced the world, and became acceptable. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰ ਘਰ ਮਹਲਾ ਹਸਤੀ ਘੋੜੇ ਛੋਡਿ ਵਿਲਾਇਤਿ ਦੇਸ ਗਏ ॥
lines.translit: dar ghar mahalaa hasatee ghorre chhodd vilaaeit des ge |
lines.english: Leaving behind hearth and home, palaces, elephants, horses and native lands, mortals have journeyed to foreign lands.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਦ ਸਹਜ ਸੁਖ ਰਸ ਕਸ ਤਜੀਅਲੇ ਕਾਪੜ ਛੋਡੇ ਚਮੜ ਲੀਏ ॥
lines.translit: saad sehaj sukh ras kas tajeeale kaaparr chhodde chamarr lee |
lines.english: Renouncing tasty delicacies, comfort, happiness and pleasures, some have abandoned their clothes and now wear skins.

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਲੜੀ ਖਪਰੀ ਲਕੜੀ ਚਮੜੀ ਸਿਖਾ ਸੂਤੁ ਧੋਤੀ ਕੀਨੑੀ ॥
lines.translit: khalarree khaparee lakarree chamarree sikhaa soot dhotee keenaee |
lines.english: Some wear skins, and carry begging bowls, bearing wooden staffs, and sitting on deer skins. Others raise their hair in tufts and wear sacred threads and loin-cloths.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੁਖੀਏ ਦਰਦਵੰਦ ਦਰਿ ਤੇਰੈ ਨਾਮਿ ਰਤੇ ਦਰਵੇਸ ਭਏ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: dukhee daradavand dar terai naam rate daraves bhe |3|
lines.english: Those who suffer in pain, imbued with Your Name, have become beggars at Your Door. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੂੰ ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਹਉ ਸਾਂਗੀ ਤੇਰਾ ਪ੍ਰਣਵੈ ਨਾਨਕੁ ਜਾਤਿ ਕੈਸੀ ॥੪॥੧॥੩੩॥
lines.translit: toon saahib hau saangee teraa pranavai naanak jaat kaisee |4|1|33|
lines.english: You are the Lord Master, I am just Your puppet. Prays Nanak, what is my social status to be? ||4||1||33||
